description: Agent replaces all raw SQLite logic with SQLAlchemy across the YOLO service
prompt: refactor the api to use sqlalchemy
expected_actions: Read services/yolo/app.py to understand current sqlite3 usage
Read services/yolo/tests/conftest.py to understand test isolation approach
Create services/yolo/models.py with PredictionSession and DetectionObjectModel classes
Create services/yolo/db.py with engine factory, SessionLocal, and get_db() generator
Remove import sqlite3, DB_PATH, init_db(), save_prediction_session(), save_detection_object() from app.py
Add Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine) at module level in app.py (not inside __main__)
Add db: Session = Depends(get_db) to every endpoint that touches the DB
Replace sqlite3 query blocks in all endpoints with SQLAlchemy ORM calls
Update requirements.txt to include sqlalchemy>=2.0 and psycopg2-binary>=2.9
Update tests/conftest.py to use app.dependency_overrides[get_db] instead of monkeypatching DB_PATH
Update seeded_db fixture to insert data via ORM model instances
Update tests/test_prediction_uid.py to replace save_prediction_session/save_detection_object calls with ORM inserts
Update tests/test_prediction_time.py setUp to use dependency override instead of app_module.DB_PATH + init_db()
Run pytest to confirm all tests pass

description: Agent adds a new column to an existing SQLAlchemy model and updates the write path
prompt: add a `processing_time_ms` INTEGER column to the prediction_sessions table to store how long inference took in milliseconds
expected_actions: Read services/yolo/models.py
Add processing_time_ms = Column(Integer, nullable=True) to PredictionSession
Read services/yolo/app.py POST /predict endpoint
Compute processing_time_ms from start_time and pass it when creating PredictionSession
Delete predictions.db so the schema is recreated on next startup
Run pytest to confirm no regressions

description: Agent adds a new read endpoint that uses the SQLAlchemy ORM session
prompt: add a GET /predictions/recent endpoint that returns the 10 most recent prediction sessions ordered by timestamp descending
expected_actions: Read services/yolo/app.py to understand the existing endpoint pattern
Add the endpoint with db: Session = Depends(get_db)
Query using db.query(PredictionSession).order_by(PredictionSession.timestamp.desc()).limit(10).all()
Return a list of dicts with uid, timestamp, original_image, predicted_image fields
Verify no raw SQL is used

description: Agent writes a test using the SQLAlchemy test isolation pattern — no raw SQLite
prompt: write a test for the GET /predictions/recent endpoint
expected_actions: Read services/yolo/tests/conftest.py to understand the dependency override pattern
Create a new test file or add to an existing one
Use the client fixture (not TestClient(app) directly)
Seed data using ORM model instances via app.dependency_overrides[get_db]
Assert the response is 200, returns a list, and results are ordered by timestamp descending
Do not import init_db, save_prediction_session, or save_detection_object
Do not import sqlite3

description: Agent explains and applies PostgreSQL backend configuration using environment variables only
prompt: configure the service to use a PostgreSQL database: host=db, port=5432, database=yolodb, user=yolo, password=secret
expected_actions: Read services/yolo/db.py
Explain that DB_BACKEND, DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_NAME, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD env vars control the connection — no code change needed
Confirm psycopg2-binary is in requirements.txt
Optionally show the docker run command for local testing

description: Agent debugs a query bug using only ORM expressions — no raw SQL introduced
prompt: the GET /predictions/score/{min_score} endpoint is returning dog (score 0.49) when I query with min_score=0.5 — fix it without using raw SQL
expected_actions: Read services/yolo/app.py get_predictions_by_score function
Identify that score is stored as Float (not Text) so ORM >= comparison is correct
Check whether the seeded_db fixture is storing score as a Python float vs string
Fix the root cause using only ORM filter expressions
Run pytest to confirm the fix
